title: SACRAMENTO ― A proposed law to prevent more fentanyl overdose deaths passed out of the Assembly Health Committee with bipartisan support. Authored by Assemblymember. Liz Ortega (D-
prompt_preview:

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Fentanyl is now the leading cause of fatal poisoning among children under five.",
    "In 2021, annual overdose deaths in the United States reached more than 100,000.",
    "Fentanyl is the leading cause of death among 18- to 45-year-olds in the United States.",
    "In California, 7,175 Californians died of overdose in 2021, an increase of 119% over 2019.",
    "Over 150 people per day are dying in the US from opioid poisoning, with fentanyl being the deadliest.",
    "Fentanyl is 50 to 100 times as potent as heroin.",
    "Sixty percent of pills seized by the DEA and tested for fentanyl contained at least 2 mg of fentanyl, considered a potentially lethal dose.",
    "Carfentanil is a hundred times more potent than fentanyl.",
    "Naloxone, recently approved for over-the-counter sale, can reverse a fentanyl or carfentanil overdose in minutes.",
    "Naloxone can cost $100 or more depending on dosage and source.",
    "AB 1060 would potentially result in the reversal of over 5,000 overdoses.",
    "The projected cost of saving over 5,000 lives with naloxone is approximately $1,800 per life saved.",
    "The average cost of an E.R. visit in California is $2,900."
  ]
}
```
